description: Quest chains shown on the /onboarding page. Data-driven so new quests can be added without touching the page code. Progress is tracked per step in the user's localStorage under the key 'humanity_quest_progress'.

chains.title: Welcome Quest
chains.description: Your first ten minutes on HumanityOS.

steps.title: Open the Chat
steps.description: Pick a name, say hi in #welcome. Your identity key is created automatically the first time.

steps.title: Fill in your Profile
steps.description: Add a display name and a short bio. This is how others will see you across the network.

steps.title: Back up your seed phrase
steps.description: Write down the 24-word seed in Settings. It is the only way to recover your identity if you lose this device.

steps.title: Try the Real / Sim toggle
steps.description: The pill in the top-right swaps between your real-life data and the simulation. Click it once to see the difference.

chains.title: Explore the Platform
chains.description: One small task for each core page. Skim-friendly, takes about fifteen minutes.

steps.title: Create a task
steps.description: Open the kanban board and add one card. Real or Sim. Something you actually want to track.

steps.title: Browse the Marketplace
steps.description: See what people are offering. Check a listing, read a review, message a seller if something catches your eye.

steps.title: Open your Wallet
steps.description: Your key is your wallet address. Copy it, share it, or send a small amount of SOL to a friend.

steps.title: Check the Maps page
steps.description: See what is around you. Real mode shows the real world. Sim mode shows the game world.

steps.title: Write a note
steps.description: Notes are encrypted on your device. Useful for journals, drafts, or anything you want private.

chains.title: Going Deeper
chains.description: When you are ready to get more out of the platform, or help build it.

steps.title: Install the desktop app
steps.description: Native binary that works offline. Same identity as the web version. Syncs when you reconnect.

steps.title: Join a federated server
steps.description: Other communities run their own servers. Your identity works on all of them. Find one whose culture fits.

steps.title: Run your own server
steps.description: One small binary, under ten minutes from zero to live. Your users keep their identity when they switch servers.

steps.title: Contribute to the project
steps.description: Code, writing, design, translation, testing, feedback. The onboarding guide shows where to start.

steps.title: Read the Humanity Accord
steps.description: The philosophical foundation of the project. Short, plain-language, not a legal document.
